title: podman run --sysctl completion for sysctl test flaky
body:
```
not ok 420 |600| podman run --sysctl completion for sysctl in 1004ms
         # tags: ci:parallel
         # (from function `bail-now' in file test/system/[helpers.bash, line 230](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/edaa158b9237a1d73361c0a671b5824acc9cdf85/test/system/helpers.bash#L230),
         #  from function `assert' in file test/system/[helpers.bash, line 1110](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/edaa158b9237a1d73361c0a671b5824acc9cdf85/test/system/helpers.bash#L1110),
         #  in test file test/system/[600-completion.bats, line 420](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/edaa158b9237a1d73361c0a671b5824acc9cdf85/test/system/600-completion.bats#L420))
         #   `assert "$output" =~ "^net\." \' failed
         #
<+     > # # podman __completeNoDesc
         #
         # run --sysctl net.
<+118ms> # :1
         # Completion ended with directive: ShellCompDirectiveError
         # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #|     FAIL: Only suggestions with 'net.' should be present for podman run --sysctl net.
         # #| expected: =~ \^net\\.
         # #|   actual:    :1
         # #|         >    Completion ended with directive: ShellCompDirectiveError
         # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
         # # [teardown]
```

Just seen once https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5301982715445248/html/sys-podman-debian-13-root-host-sqlite.log.html
But that definitely looks like something which should never flake

comments.body: I've observed this flake multiple times, mostly on Debian 13. The issue is caused by `/proc/sys` being a volatile virtual filesystem where entries can disappear between directory enumeration and file access.
